summary: Fair soundboard capture of a historic May‑24‑1969 Miami show – some standout jams but overall an average performance.
review: The recording, sourced from a soundboard (unknown quality), is listenable but suffers from cuts, a brief pitch wobble and missing bits that keep it from being pristine. Musically the concert offers a few high points—a unique Jerry jam early on and a solid Feedback—but much of the set drifts, with shaky Doin' That Rag, a draggy Just a Hand to Hold, a loose Alligator and an unfocused drum segment. Fans who value historic context will enjoy it, though serious listeners may find it only middling.

ai_rating.rationale: Three out of four reviewers gave high star ratings, but the detailed review highlights numerous performance and technical flaws; weighting show (70%) and recording (30%) yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Contains a ~36 sec cut, a pitch drop of about –2% during a solo, and some missing seconds at the start/end of tracks; overall clearer than audience tapes but not pristine.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first half contains the best jam of the night and a brief unique Jerry segment, but the set quickly loses momentum; overall it feels average for a 1969 show with occasional high‑water moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Brief unique jam noted around 13:45; otherwise not highlighted.
band_member_comments.Drums: Described as "shoes in the dryer" with little cohesion or interplay.
